title: Repository Webhook Health
app: GitHub Webhook deliveries API, Splunk HEC receiver logs
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="github:webhook_delivery"
| where status_code>=400 OR delivered="false"
| stats count by repository, hook_id, status_code
| where count > 5
| sort -count
description: Failed webhook deliveries break CI, security scanning, and Splunk ingestion‚Äîmonitor delivery logs and HTTP status.
value: Failed webhook deliveries break CI, security scanning, and Splunk ingestion‚Äîmonitor delivery logs and HTTP status.
implementation: Poll recent deliveries or ingest GitHub Enterprise audit. Alert on sustained 4xx/5xx to Splunk HEC. Verify TLS cert on endpoint.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: GitHub Webhook deliveries API, Splunk HEC receiver logs.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `delivery` records with `status_code`, `error_message`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Poll recent deliveries or ingest GitHub Enterprise audit. Alert on sustained 4xx/5xx to Splunk HEC. Verify TLS cert on endpoint.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="github:webhook_delivery"
| where status_code>=400 OR delivered="false"
| stats count by repository, hook_id, status_code
| where count > 5
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Repository Webhook Health** ‚Äî Failed webhook deliveries break CI, security scanning, and Splunk ingestion‚Äîmonitor delivery logs and HTTP status.

Documented **Data sources**: `delivery` records with `status_code`, `error_message`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): GitHub Webhook deliveries API, Splunk HEC receiver logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops; **sourcetype**: github:webhook_delivery. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=devops, sourcetype="github:webhook_delivery". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where status_code>=400 OR delivered="false"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by repository, hook_id, status_code** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where count > 5` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failing hooks), Line chart (failure rate), Single value (open incidents).
visualization: Table (failing hooks), Line chart (failure rate), Single value (open incidents).
